channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistani Politician Accuses Army of Concealing Kargil War Casualties
item.description: Maulana Fazal-ur-Rehman, president of the Jamiat Ulema-e-Islam, accused the Pakistan Army of concealing casualties during the 1999 Kargil War. He claimed that Islamabad did not publicly release the li...

item.title: Family finds closure as Chinnadu's killer is convicted in 'car noise' murder case
item.description: Kubendran Sholan Chinnadu, a 29-year-old electrician from Northdale, was tragically shot and killed in Pietermaritzburg in July 2020 following a dispute over 'car noise'. The incident occurred when Ch...

item.title: Philippines and US Discuss Regional Cooperation Ahead of ASEAN Meetings
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. received United States Ambassador to India Sergio Gor and US Ambassador to the Philippines Lee Lipton at Malacañang Palace ahead of ASEAN-related engagements in Manila. ...

item.title: Five family members, including a Utah fire captain, killed in flash flooding near Bicknell
item.description: Five family members, including Provo fire captain Spencer Long and his wife Katrina, along with their three sons Reid, Thayne, and Gage, tragically died in a flash flood near Bicknell, Utah. The incid...

item.title: Trump's Qatari jet security concerns emerge after conflicting statements and legal actions
item.description: Jen Psaki, a journalist for MS NOW, highlighted President Donald Trump's inconsistent statements regarding the security of his Qatari-gifted Air Force One. Initially, Trump denied any safety concerns ...

item.title: U.S.-Iran Conflict Escalates Amid Airstrikes, Oil Supply Risks and Regional Tensions
item.description: The article details the ongoing escalation of tensions between the United States and Iran, marked by a series of airstrikes targeting Iranian infrastructure and retaliatory strikes from Tehran. U.S. P...

item.title: 10-year-old boy's death highlights systemic hospital transfer failures
item.description: A 10-year-old boy's death has exposed critical failures in the hospital transfer system, according to a recent report. The incident occurred after prolonged delays in transferring the child to a speci...

item.title: House Committee Investigates PFIPC Allegations Amid Budget Concerns
item.description: The House of Representatives ad hoc committee has started investigating the controversial Presidential Foreign Investment Promotion Council (PFIPC), which lawmakers claim was illegally included in the...

item.title: Liberal Scepticism and the Paralysis of Oppositional Politics in India
item.description: The article critiques the current state of Indian politics, highlighting how liberal scepticism has led to a collapse in the ability to distinguish truth from fiction among opposition groups. It argue...

item.title: Spain's Defensive Mastery Secures 2026 FIFA World Cup Victory
item.description: Spain claimed their second FIFA World Cup title in 2026, defeating Argentina 1-0 in extra time at MetLife Stadium. The victory solidified their status as European champions and marked the first time a...

item.title: Nigeria Focuses on Legal Clarity to Attract Energy Investors Amid Net-Zero Goals
item.description: The Nigerian petroleum ministry is prioritizing regulatory certainty over financial incentives to attract oil and gas investors, ensuring the country can achieve net-zero targets without disrupting it...

item.title: Women with PMOS face four times higher heart disease risk, study finds
item.description: A study published in The Lancet Obstetrics, Gynaecology, & Women’s Health has revealed that women with polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome (PMOS) face a fourfold increased risk of developing hear...

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Threatens Gulf Coast with Flooding and Strong Winds
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha has formed in the northeastern Gulf of Mexico and is expected to impact portions of the Gulf Coast from Florida to Louisiana, potentially reaching as far west as Texas. The storm...

item.title: KPMG Appoints New CEO Amid Job Cuts and Scandal Fallout
item.description: KPMG Australia has appointed John Sams as its new chief executive, effective immediately, as the firm prepares for significant workforce reductions following a whistleblower scandal that has damaged i...

item.title: Zanzibar Hosts Thousands at FIFA World Cup Final Fan Zone
item.description: The Zanzibar Fan Zone campaign concluded with a major event at Kisonge, where thousands of football fans gathered to watch the 2026 FIFA World Cup final between Spain and Argentina. Organized by digit...

item.title: Federal court directs Edmonton Institution for Women to improve temperature monitoring and pursue air conditioning in secure unit
item.description: A Federal Court judge has ordered Correctional Service Canada (CSC) to take concrete steps to address longstanding concerns about extreme heat in the secure unit at the Edmonton Institution for Women....

item.title: AUKUS Inquiry in Adelaide Sparks Debate Over Security and Environmental Concerns
item.description: The article details the ongoing AUKUS inquiry in Adelaide, focusing on the potential environmental and security implications of the alliance's initiatives. Experts and stakeholders are scrutinizing th...

item.title: Border Czar Tom Homan defends ICE actions amid controversy over fatal incidents
item.description: Tom Homan, President Donald Trump's border czar, sparked controversy during a press briefing at the White House by making contentious claims about ICE-related fatalities. Discussing the deaths of Lore...

item.title: Global loneliness is widespread, but research shows a more complex picture than an epidemic
item.description: New research examining data from more than 218,000 people across nearly 150 countries challenges the idea that the world is experiencing a simple and universal loneliness epidemic. The study found tha...

item.title: New Orleans Police Department Releases Draft Policy Allowing Weaponized Drones, Despite Current Ban
item.description: A draft New Orleans Police Department (NOPD) drone manual, briefly published online, would have allowed police drones to carry weapons with written approval from the superintendent, according to 404 M...
